message:
fix: preserve sub-second precision in SQLite export timestamps

Change export_sqlite.rs timestamp formatting from SecondsFormat::Secs
to SecondsFormat::Micros. The Secs format truncated nanosecond-precision
timestamps from the JSONL source, losing data fidelity in the SQLite
export. Micros preserves precision while still using the Z suffix.

The robot.rs hash computation and TUI display locations intentionally
keep Secs — the hash only needs consistency (not precision), and the
TUI display benefits from brevity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
